name: Soft Shell Crabs in Black Bean & Chili Sauce
recipeIngredient: 2 tablespoons Salted black beans: rinsed in hot water, drained, and mashed into a paste
2 Dried red chili peppers, torn into small pieces; do not discard seeds
1 Scallion, cut into pea-sized pieces, including the green part
1/2 cup Green or red bell pepper, chopped
1 1/2 tablespoons Chinese Shaoyang wine or pale dry sherry
1 tablespoon Cornstarch mixed with 2 tablespoons cold water
recipeInstructions:
1. Heat 1 cup oil in a Wok until it reaches deep-frying temperature. Add crabs and deep fry until they turn orange. Remove the crabs and drain them; discard the oil. Clean and dry the Wok.
2. Heat the Wok over high heat. Swirl in 2 tablespoons oil. When the oil is hot, add seasonings. Stir fry for about 30 seconds. Add bell pepper and stir fry for about 10 seconds. Stir in the sauce mixture. Return the cooked crabs to the Wok. Cover and turn the heat to low. Cook until the sauce comes to a boil. Stir in cornstarch and water. Continue stirring until the sauce is thickened. Serve on a plate.
NOTE: For a less pungent hot dish, use 1 tablespoon salted black beans and 1 dried chili pepper.
When you buy soft shell crabs, ensure they are still alive. They may appear dead or weak but will respond slightly when touched. Refrigerate them as soon as possible after purchase.
Cleaning the crabs is straightforward:
- Clip off the eyes and mouth.
- Turn the crab upside down and remove the soft feather-like gills.
- Remove the cartilage (the female's shape is half-oval, and the male's is a narrow triangle).
